title: Exostema floribundum
intro: Exostema floribundum, also known as the Jamaican dogwood, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Its health benefits include the potential to reduce anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative properties, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and alleviate pain. The bioactive constituents of Exostema floribundum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and iridoids, such as exostemin and exostemine, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Exostema floribundum is often used to make tinctures, teas, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including insomnia, anxiety, and hypertension.
taxonomy: Exostema floribundum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the madder or coffee family. The Rubiaceae family is a large group of flowering plants that includes approximately 13,900 species, many of which are shrubs or small trees. Exostema floribundum is native to Central America and is a member of the Rubieae tribe within the Rubiaceae family. It is a shrub or small tree with clusters of small, white or yellow flowers.

In some cultures, the plant is used to treat digestive problems and as an antiseptic. Additionally, it is also believed to have antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential.
